item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's hard to write a good funny SCP, but you've managed. The part where the MTF leader reminds everyone to be careful and then immediately gets swatted in the face … to no ill effect, unless you count embarrassment … was the highlight of the writeup. I can imagine some people would criticize your characterization of MTF dynamics as too silly, but honestly, I'd have trouble taking my job seriously if it sent me into a gigantic pillow fort / mattress castle. I'm actually glad the SCP didn't go in a "thing what kills you (when you least expect it)" direction; it's easier to accept that this scip simply exists in a canon where most anomalies are nonlethal enough to excuse letting one's guard down, at least in circumstances that seem more oddball than creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The title is so good. And the narrative and situation itself is really freaky and fun.

item.title: This is great
item.content:encoded:
Easily one of the funniest SCPs that isn't a -J on this site. Even all these years later, I still love throwing pillows, so this played very well into my dreams.

+1.

item.title: I think I might have found one of my favourite Series VI SCPs
item.content:encoded:
I ate my mattress after I saw that pic.

Then I read the SCP anyways and absolutely loved it.

Especially at the mental image of trained soldiers pillow fighting plush people.

+1.

Wish I could +9001.

I like the premise, and the article kept my interest all the way through. I did have some issues during the audio transcript with the writing and pacing, but overall it doesn't detract too much from the article. Good job on this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was 100% down with this at the beginning, but the transcript dragged for a bit and felt a little… tired? I've seen a fair few anomalies made of things where the twist is that you need to fight them with that thing, such that the initial conflict seems a little manufactured.

Ending was humorously anti-climatic though. I'll give this an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-5126-A is Josh Gad and nobody can convince me otherwise.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: I feel like the grammar could use some work, for example : The object lost during the event is to be replaced. This does not really work as its own scentence.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
NOW EAT YOUR MATTRESS

Entry for Jamcon day 2.

I didn't have time to get quite as much crit as I would've liked on this unfortunately. Either way thanks to HunterDog and Popsioak for crit.

Edit: Image is created by myself.
